My reason for abandon  was that this was strictly theoretical. As 1B(10 digits) was way to large for any practical PCC rules. (I shoud have asked)

Patch Set 4:

It isn't just whether this is an unrealistic argument, but also whether an incorrect value can crash the kernel or not.

parents.subject: LU-17658 fid: check on disk sequence before allocating to osp

commit.subject: LU-17000 llite: Handle not NUL terminated buffer
commit.message:
LU-17000 llite: Handle not NUL terminated buffer

In pcc_expr_time_parse() 'buf' may not have a null
terminator if the source string's length is equal
to the buffer. This patch handles this.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-pcc
CoverityID: 426259 ("Buffer not null terminated")
Fixes: 3835f4d3 (LU-13881 pcc: comparator support for PCC rules)
Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Change-Id: Ifc144d73c75b8eef25a994630c600b9c1922aa3b
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55377
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
